ground.what_this_is: This is a design probe for a 'Portal' feature, showing how a window onto another address should look on the desktop interface.
ground.what_you_can_do: You can view the proposed 'live window' style (a translucent frame) and the sidebar list of nodes; you cannot interact with the mockup as it is a static design document.

howto.how_to_change: It lives in the toolbar region (canvas/app/src/regions/Toolbar.tsx); adding or changing a control is a code change there, surfaced via the build-intent wire.
howto.what: The layers control on the toolbar.
howto.what_you_can_do: Open the layers panel to manage the system's structural depth.

howto.how_to_change: It lives in the toolbar region (canvas/app/src/regions/Toolbar.tsx); changing its behavior is a code change there, surfaced via the build-intent wire.
howto.what: The wire command button on the bridge's command strip.
howto.what_you_can_do: Initiate a wire to the system's intent layer, translating the current board state into a proposed action for the network.

howto.how_to_change: It lives in the toolbar region (canvas/app/src/regions/Toolbar.tsx); changing it is a code change there, surfaced via the build-intent wire.
howto.what: The run command on the toolbar.
howto.what_you_can_do: Initiate the system's active execution state.

howto.how_to_change: The rack fills itself from the live registry; adding a node-type (nodes/<name>.py) makes a new block appear here automatically, no palette code change required.
howto.what: The 'Node palette' — the rack of build-blocks displaying every known node-type (grouped by kind: process, acts, content, holds).
howto.what_you_can_do: Click a block to drop that node on the board; select two and use the wire tool to connect them.

howto.how_to_change: Mockup-only; no built code path maps to this specific text yet.
howto.what: The memo gate control on a node card, explaining the runtime optimization rule.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the explanation that the node skips re-running when its hash is unchanged; this is a static informational probe in the mockup.

howto.how_to_change: The visible text and logic live in the node's specific implementation file (nodes/<name>.py); the generic card shape is NodeShape.tsx.
howto.what: The memo gate card on a node, displaying the source artefact model and the memo gate logic.
howto.what_you_can_do: View the source artefact model (e.g., deepseek-v4-pro) and read the memo gate condition that skips re-running the node when its hash is unchanged.

howto.how_to_change: It is the portal node-type (nodes/portal.py); to change what it shows, edit the target address in its config.ref setting.
howto.what: A live mirror window onto a remote address, showing the current value and its hash.
howto.what_you_can_do: View the live state of the target address (run://demo/answer) and its integrity hash; changes to the source update this view instantly.

howto.how_to_change: It is a mockup-only design probe; no built code path exists yet to change this state.
howto.what: The resolution status indicator for a portal window.
howto.what_you_can_do: View whether the portal's reference target is currently resolving or has failed to resolve.

howto.how_to_change: Mockup-only; no built code path maps to this specific label yet.
howto.what: The address label displayed in the inspector panel, indicating the node's current configuration reference.
howto.what_you_can_do: View the address this window reflects; it serves as a static label for the node's configuration state.

howto.how_to_change: This is a design probe/mockup; it is not yet built. If built, it would live in the Inspector.tsx region, displaying the result of the node's state evaluation logic.
howto.what: The status indicator on the inspector panel showing whether a node's state is computed or static.
howto.what_you_can_do: View the current state classification of the selected node (specifically that it is 'not a computed status').

howto.how_to_change: Mockup-only; no built code path maps to this specific panel yet. Changes would require creating a new region in the canvas shell and wiring it to the portal reference logic.
howto.what: The Inspector panel on the canvas, acting as a live window onto the portal node's reference address.
howto.what_you_can_do: View the live state of the portal node (content, reference address, and resolution status) without triggering a re-run; observe how the window reflects the same content-hash as the run state.

howto.how_to_change: Mockup-only; no built code path maps to this feature yet.
howto.what: The portal window on the canvas header, offering a view into another address.
howto.what_you_can_do: View the proposed live window style (a translucent frame) and the sidebar list of nodes; note that interaction is currently disabled as this is a static design document.

reasons: accuracy jury (Layer 2, SOFT — variance not error): no quorum that the dossier is grounded in the element (0 of 3 draws agreed grounded). Flagged for operator scrutiny.

howto.how_to_change: The list and labels are driven by the NODE-config and NODE-kinds definitions in the canvas region; structural changes to the palette's layout or available kinds require edits to the canvas region code.
howto.what: The node palette sidebar on the canvas, listing available node kinds and their configurations.
howto.what_you_can_do: Select a node kind (e.g., Content, constant) to add it to the canvas; view its specific configuration details (e.g., model_of_tim, rhm_mode) without altering the live state.

reasons: accuracy jury (Layer 2, SOFT — variance not error): no quorum that the dossier is grounded in the element (0 of 3 draws agreed grounded). Flagged for operator scrutiny.

howto.how_to_change: It lives in the canvas region (canvas/app/src/NodeShape.tsx); changing its visible text or data-kind requires a code edit there.
howto.what: A process node on the live composition board.
howto.what_you_can_do: Select it to inspect its model (deepseek-v4-pro) and read the memo gate status; it reflects the current runtime state without triggering a re-run.

reasons: accuracy jury (Layer 2, SOFT — variance not error): no quorum that the dossier is grounded in the element (1 of 3 draws agreed grounded). Flagged for operator scrutiny.

howto.how_to_change: It lives in the portal region (nodes/portal.py); the logic is driven by runtime/scheduler.py. Changing the behavior requires modifying the scheduler logic or the node's hash calculation.
howto.what: The memo gate — a status indicator on the portal window showing when a node's hash is stable and re-running is skipped.
howto.what_you_can_do: View the system's optimization logic: see that the node (type, version, config, inputs) hash is unchanged, so the runtime skips re-running.

reasons: accuracy jury (Layer 2, SOFT — variance not error): no quorum that the dossier is grounded in the element (1 of 3 draws agreed grounded). Flagged for operator scrutiny.

howto.how_to_change: It lives in the canvas region (canvas/app/src/NodeShape.tsx) wired to the source's output; changing the frame style or connection logic is a code edit there.
howto.what: The live window frame that opens onto another address (e.g., run://demo/answer).
howto.what_you_can_do: View the remote content as a live, translucent overlay; the frame reflects the source's state without copying it.

howto.what_you_can_do: View the system's live state summary (running, thinking, awaiting) and the current event stream status; this is a static design probe and cannot be interacted with.

howto.how_to_change: It lives in the Inspector.tsx region; changing the resolve logic requires a code update there.
howto.what: The resolve control on the inspector panel.
howto.what_you_can_do: Mark a node as resolved, which signals the scheduler to skip further reads and prevents the run() function from being called.
